notes: Iteration 2 adds 3 new security evals (5, 6, 7) testing path validation: load-path-traversal, load-absolute-path, list-path-traversal
The new skill passes all 3 path safety evals at 100% — it rejects traversal paths, absolute paths, and traversal in list scans
The old skill scores 0% on load-path-traversal and load-absolute-path: it has NO path validation and blindly attempts to read whatever path is provided
The old skill scores 33% on list-path-traversal only because it lacks a list command entirely, not because of any security check — accidental safety, not by design
Eval 3 (load-rejected-plan) drops to 67% for both configurations because the 'if proceeding' expectation is unverifiable in a single-turn eval — both skills correctly stop at the confirmation prompt
Evals 1-4 show consistent results with iteration 1: the new skill preserves full relative paths in metadata while the old skill loses path information
The new skill is significantly faster (mean 42.0s vs 70.5s) and uses fewer tokens (mean 12483 vs 14086) — the old skill spends extra tokens on failed attempts and workarounds
